expectations.text: A regression test for the specific bug is written BEFORE any fix is applied
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows 'Creating initial failing test' (msg 8) before 'Switching to float parsing' (msg 11). Test created first.

expectations.text: The regression test name clearly describes the bug (e.g., TestParseDuration_HandlesFractionalHours)
expectations.evidence: Test named TestParseDuration_FractionalHours — same as with_skill version.

expectations.text: The regression test is run and confirmed to FAIL before the fix
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows 'Running initial tests to confirm failure' (msg 9) before fix.

expectations.text: The fix uses strconv.ParseFloat instead of strconv.Atoi (as the bug description suggests)
expectations.evidence: duration.go line 39: strconv.ParseFloat(numStr, 64) — identical fix to with_skill.

expectations.text: The fix is minimal — only changes what's needed to handle fractional values
expectations.evidence: Identical minimal fix as with_skill. Only changes strconv.Atoi → ParseFloat and adjusts Duration math.

expectations.text: Existing behaviors are preserved (integer durations like '2h' still work)
expectations.evidence: ParseFloat handles integers correctly. Same reasoning as with_skill.

expectations.text: Additional edge cases are tested (e.g., '0.5m', '2.25h', fractional seconds)
expectations.evidence: Only one test: TestParseDuration_FractionalHours for '1.5h'. No additional edge case tests.

expectations.text: All tests pass after the fix
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows 'Running tests on changed files' (msg 13) and 'Marking all complete' (msg 16).
